description: Browser-side storage attacks — localStorage XSS persistence, ServiceWorker hijack, IndexedDB tampering, BFCache (back-forward cache) auth bypass, quota-exceeded fallback. These are CLIENT-SIDE bugs but reportable when chained or when the app trusts client-stored data.
craft_guidance: 1. INVENTORY: open DevTools -> Application tab. Note localStorage / sessionStorage / IndexedDB stores / Service Workers / Cache Storage.
2. TRUST BOUNDARIES: which client-stored values does the app TRUST? auth tokens, role, csrf seeds, feature flags, config URLs.
3. WRITE PRIMITIVES: what allows writing storage? Reflected XSS, stored XSS, postMessage handler, location.hash parser, OAuth state parameter.
4. PERSISTENCE: localStorage / IndexedDB persist. Service Workers persist + intercept. Cache Storage persists + serves intercepted requests.
5. SERVICE WORKER FETCH HIJACK: if attacker registers /sw.js (via stored XSS or path-traversal upload), SW intercepts ALL same-origin requests for ~24h.
6. BFCACHE: back-button restores tabs WITHOUT re-running auth checks. If app sets sensitive state on render, victim's back-button into logged-in state after logout.
7. STORAGE EVENT XSS: localStorage 'storage' event fires across tabs. Poisoned localStorage executes when victim opens another tab.
8. INDEXEDDB: structured storage; many apps use it as cache for API responses. Tampering = serve poisoned content from local cache.
9. QUOTA DOS: fill localStorage to quota; some apps fall back to in-memory or sync XHR (DoS).
10. PARTITION: top-level vs. third-party iframe storage. CHIPS / partitioned cookies don't apply to storage; iframe attacks may share storage with parent.

service_worker_hijack.description: Register attacker-controlled /sw.js (via stored XSS, file upload to webroot, or path traversal) — Service Worker intercepts all subsequent same-origin requests including those that contain auth tokens.

probes.description: Check if /sw.js is reachable + cacheable. If 200 + cache-control allows long TTL, hijack window is large.
[Manual] If you have a write primitive at /sw.js, this SW exfiltrates every request URL to Collaborator while still serving the response. Replace COLLABORATOR.

localstorage_xss_persistence.description: XSS payload stored in localStorage; victim revisits in a new tab; payload re-executes from storage. Persists across page reloads even if the original XSS sink is patched.

probes.description: Check whether app reads JSON from localStorage and renders without sanitization. Search response JS for `JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(`.
Search for `innerHTML\s*=\s*localStorage` or template-render with localStorage value as input. These are XSS sinks where the payload survives reload.

indexeddb_tampering.description: Apps that cache API responses in IndexedDB and serve the cached content for offline mode are vulnerable to IndexedDB tampering. Attacker writes crafted record; app loads and trusts.

probes.description: Detect IndexedDB usage. Search response JS for `indexedDB.open` and identify the schema (database / object store names).

bfcache_auth_bypass.description: Browsers cache full page state in BFCache. After logout, back-button restores the in-memory authenticated state including JS variables holding tokens. Server-side auth check on next request may pass if cookies survive (HttpOnly) or fail silently.

probes.description: Check Cache-Control on logout response. If it doesn't include `no-store`, BFCache may restore the authenticated page after back navigation.

cache_storage_poisoning.description: Service Worker often pre-caches assets via Cache Storage. Attacker who controls a CDN, MITM, or an upstream resource can poison cached entries and persist across sessions.

probes.description: Inspect SW for cache.put with non-cors-validated origins. Search for `caches.open(...).then(c=>c.put(req, response))` patterns where response origin isn't validated.

storage_event_cross_tab.description: localStorage 'storage' event fires in OTHER tabs sharing the origin. Attacker tab writes payload; victim tab on same origin reads + executes via storage handler.

probes.description: Search for `addEventListener('storage'` handlers that pass values to innerHTML/eval/Function.

quota_exceeded_fallback.description: App falls back to insecure storage (in-memory variable, document.cookie) when localStorage is full. Attacker fills quota then attacker iframe can read fallback storage.

probes.description: Search response JS for try/catch around `localStorage.setItem` with cookie/in-memory fallback.
